title: Stanzas On The Taking Of Quebec, And Death Of General Wolfe
content:
Amidst the clamour of exulting joys,
    Which triumph forces from the patriot heart,
    Grief dares to mingle her soul-piercing voice,
    And quells the raptures which from pleasures start.

    O WOLFE! to thee a streaming flood of woe,
    Sighing we pay, and think e'en conquest dear;
    QUEBEC in vain shall teach our breast to glow,
    Whilst thy sad fate extorts the heart-wrung tear.

    Alive the foe thy dreadful vigour fled,
    And saw thee fall with joy-pronouncing eyes:
    Yet they shall know thou conquerest, though dead 
    Since from thy tomb a thousand heroes rise!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Oliver Goldsmith delivers a powerful performance in "Stanzas On The Taking Of Quebec, And Death Of General Wolfe"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
